operator_scope.semantic_signature: KBX layout correctness failure from contiguous-only, view-only, unique-index, dense-mask, or wrong-scatter assumptions

valid_when: A KBX layout/indexing candidate passes simple public cases but fails hidden, non-contiguous, indexed, masked, or duplicate-index cases.
invalid_when: The failure is a compile error or pure dtype mismatch unrelated to indexing/layout/mask semantics.
preconditions: Inspect candidate pointer arithmetic, high-level layout calls, index assumptions, mask-output shape, and scatter semantics.

mechanism: Expanded KBX layout tasks deliberately include concrete operations whose correctness depends on stride-aware offsets, materialized-copy semantics, index/mask bounds, duplicate-index policy, and compact vs dense output shape.

instructions: If hidden failures appear after public contiguous cases pass, replace linear contiguous offsets with shape/stride offset calculations.
For permute_copy, verify the candidate materializes the permuted output rather than returning a view or delegating to contiguous().
For index_select/fused_gather_masked_fill, remove sorted/unique/contiguous-index assumptions and add index bounds tests.
For masked_select, check that output is compact one-dimensional as declared; for expand_where, check dense broadcasted output shape.
For scatter, verify overwrite semantics and duplicate-index policy rather than assuming scatter-add or unique indices.
action.patch_hint: Build a small correctness matrix with non-contiguous source, random index, duplicate scatter, broadcasted mask, and all-false/all-true mask before changing performance knobs.

expected_effect.correctness: Targets common hidden-correctness failures for concrete KBX layout tasks.
expected_effect.performance: No performance claim; repairs should run before tuning.
expected_effect.search_efficiency: Moves the agent away from random launch-parameter tuning when the failure is semantic.
expected_effect.risk: Some tasks may not include all boundary cases; apply only when OpSpec allows the relevant layout/index/mask behavior.

validation.promotion_policy: Candidate failure signature; promote only after repeated observed H20 failure traces across tasks.
